channel.title: DotNET on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in DotNET on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: 🔑 Easier BYOK for GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI supports Bring Your Own Key (BYOK), letting you point the CLI at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, Azure OpenAI, or Anthropic instead of the default Copilot service. The capability is powerful, but the raw workflow requires you to export several environment variables every time you switch providers. This post walks through the manual approach first, shows why it becomes cumbersome, and then introduces gh-copilot-byok, a .NET global tool that wraps the whole flow with a polished interactive interface.

item.title: ⚡ Local Energy Assistant: C# + GPT-OSS + RAG
item.description:
Introduction

Imagine having an AI assistant that understands your energy consumption patterns, solar production, and weather data — all running locally on your machine with zero cloud dependencies. No API keys, no subscription fees, no data leaving your premises.

In this hands-on guide, we’ll build a Local Energy Assistant using C#, the open-source GPT-OSS-20B model, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and custom tool calling. Perfect for privacy-conscious developers who want intelligent insights from their smart meter data.

item.title: 🚀 .NET 9 Performance in Azure: A Deep Technical Dive
item.description:
.NET 9 represents a significant leap forward in performance optimization, particularly for Azure-hosted applications. With cloud costs continuing to rise and performance expectations at an all-time high, these improvements can directly impact both your application’s user experience and your Azure bill. Let’s explore the technical depths of what makes .NET 9 a game-changer for Azure workloads.

Executive Summary: The Performance Revolution

Before diving into the technical details, here’s what .NET 9 delivers for Azure applications:
